summary: Media rendering fix verified - getMediaUrl() helper now correctly converts /uploads/ paths to /api/uploads/ for proper Kubernetes ingress routing. All images now load correctly across Customer Home, Property Detail, and Admin Inventory pages.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py - Changed static file mount from /uploads to /api/uploads
/app/backend/server.py - Updated upload endpoints to return /api/uploads/ paths
/app/frontend/src/utils/api.js - Enhanced getMediaUrl() to convert legacy /uploads/ URLs to /api/uploads/
context_for_next_testing_agent: Media rendering fix applied. The issue was that /uploads/ path was being routed to frontend instead of backend by Kubernetes ingress. Fix: (1) Backend now mounts static files at /api/uploads instead of /uploads, (2) Upload endpoints return /api/uploads/ paths, (3) getMediaUrl() converts legacy full URLs containing /uploads/ to /api/uploads/. All 9 images on Customer Home now load correctly including 2 uploaded images that were previously broken.

customer_home_images.details: All 9 images load correctly including 2 uploaded images

property_detail_images.details: Property images and thumbnails load correctly

admin_inventory_images.details: All 5 property images in inventory list load correctly

rider_dashboard.details: Dashboard loads correctly, no images shown when offline (expected)

root_cause_analysis.issue: Images with /uploads/ path were not loading (showing broken image icons)
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Kubernetes ingress routes /api/* to backend and everything else to frontend. The /uploads/ path was being served by frontend (returning HTML) instead of backend static files.
fix_applied: Changed backend static file mount from /uploads to /api/uploads
Updated all upload endpoints to return /api/uploads/ paths
Enhanced getMediaUrl() to convert legacy URLs with /uploads/ to /api/uploads/
root_cause_analysis.verification: All images now load correctly with /api/uploads/ path
